lesson_title: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.front: What is CTAD (Class Template Argument Deduction)?
cards.back: C++17 feature that allows the compiler to deduce template arguments from constructor arguments, eliminating the need to specify them explicitly.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.question: Does this compile in C++17?
cards.answer: Yes. CTAD deduces T=int from the initializer values.
cards.explanation: C++17 can deduce template arguments from constructor/initializer.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.text: With CTAD (C++17+), you can write Pair p { 5, 10 }; instead of Pair{{c1::<int>}} p { 5, 10 };
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides
13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.question: What type is deduced for p?
cards.answer: Box<double> (T is deduced as double from 3.14)
cards.explanation: CTAD uses initializer type to deduce template argument.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.back: User-defined rules that help the compiler deduce template arguments in situations where automatic deduction doesn't work or needs customization.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.text: Deduction guide syntax: Pair(T, T) -> Pair{{c1::<T>}}; (tells compiler how to deduce template args)
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.question: What does this deduction guide do?
cards.answer: Tells compiler how to deduce T when constructing Box from a single argument.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.front: Does CTAD work with aggregate initialization?
cards.back: Yes, starting in C++17, but may require deduction guides for complex cases.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.explanation:
CTAD advantages:
  • Less verbose code
  • Reduced redundancy
  • Cleaner syntax
Limitations:
  • May reduce clarity in complex cases
  • Requires C++17+
  • Sometimes needs deduction guides
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.front: Can CTAD deduce template arguments from function return types?
cards.back: No. CTAD only works with direct initialization/construction, not from return types or assignments alone.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.text: CTAD ({{c1::Class Template Argument Deduction}}) was introduced in {{c2::C++17}}.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.text: {{c1::Deduction guides}} help the compiler deduce template arguments when CTAD alone isn't sufficient.
cards.source: 13.14 — Class template argument deduction (CTAD) and deduction guides

cards.text: CTAD ({{c1::Class Template Argument Deduction}}) was introduced in {{c2::C++17}}.
